observableStarEvents.label: Perseid Meteor Shower
observableStarEvents.description: Peak Aug 12–13. Up to 100 meteors/hr. Observable from Northern Hemisphere.

observableStarEvents.label: Geminid Meteor Shower
observableStarEvents.description: Peak Dec 13–14. One of the best annual showers. 120+ meteors/hr.

observableStarEvents.label: Mars Opposition
observableStarEvents.description: Mars at opposition. Brightest and closest. Best viewing.

observableStarEvents.label: Jupiter–Saturn Conjunction
observableStarEvents.description: Jupiter and Saturn visible close together in morning sky.

gravitationalEvents.label: Total Solar Eclipse
gravitationalEvents.description: Path: Northern Russia, Arctic, Greenland, Iceland, Spain. Max duration 2m 18s.

gravitationalEvents.label: Total Solar Eclipse
gravitationalEvents.description: Eclipse of the century. 6m 23s totality. Spain, N Africa, Saudi Arabia, Yemen, Somalia. Longest until 2114.

gravitationalEvents.label: Annular Solar Eclipse
gravitationalEvents.description: Ring of fire. Path over Antarctica. Partial from S Argentina, Chile, Africa.

gravitationalEvents.label: GW231123
gravitationalEvents.description: GW231123: Most massive black hole merger ever observed. ~225 M☉ final. LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA.

gravitationalEvents.label: GW250114
gravitationalEvents.description: Loudest gravitational wave signal. SNR ~80. Two ~33 M☉ black holes. Kerr nature tests.

gravitationalEvents.label: LVK Observing Run
gravitationalEvents.description: LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA O4/O5. 200+ black hole mergers detected. Ongoing gravitational wave astronomy.
